channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: 100 days of the Iran war: How global markets and the economy have been affected, in charts
item.description: The conflict is about to reach its 100th day, with peace negotiations hanging in the balance.

item.title: U.S. eyes Iranian assets for Gulf allies' reconstruction, source says
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has also directed a team to assess the costs of damage already inflicted on Gulf allies by Iran, Reuters reported.

item.title: Chinese EVs may hit U.S. within a few years, one way or another
item.description: Chinese electric vehicles are likely to be sold in the U.S. within years, despite tariffs, strict laws and opposition from the auto industry and politicians.

item.title: Global week ahead: Soccer isn't the only thing that's kicking off
item.description: World Cup fever grips football fans worldwide ahead of the opening game on June 11th, but it's not just soccer that's kicking off next week.

item.title: Bank of America says these 'compelling' stocks are going higher
item.description: Bank of America named a slew of stocks that are too attractive to ignore.

item.title: Nvidia, SK to detail cooperation plan as Huang flags prolonged chip shortage
item.description: A SK Hynix spokesperson said group Chairman Chey Tae-won and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang plan to brief the media on Monday morning, confirming a report by Newsis.

item.title: U.S. confirms second Texas screwworm case, Canada restricts livestock imports
item.description: New World screwworm larvae feed on the living tissue of warm-blooded animals, creating severe wounds that can be fatal if left untreated.

item.title: OPEC+ set for fourth oil quota hike since Strait of Hormuz closure, sources say
item.description: Seven members of OPEC+, which groups OPEC and allied producers including Russia, increased output quotas from April to June by almost 600,000 barrels per day.

item.title: Pope Leo tells 1.2 million crowd in Madrid that God stands with the poor
item.description: Leo said he hoped the visit would set an example to the world about respecting "every human being" and urged leaders to stop dividing electorates.

item.title: These are the most overbought stocks after a volatile week
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise was among this week's most overbought stocks.

item.title: Warren Buffett disciple Guy Spier built career on value investing. A rare cancer changed everything
item.description: After a glioblastoma diagnosis, value investor Guy Spier rethought wealth, time and legacy — and the impact he can have on rare disease research.

item.title: Remote work career risks for Gen Z are 'subtle, but material,' Harvard professor says: How to avoid the pitfalls
item.description: Forgoing the office can have detrimental "scarring" impacts on young workers. Here's how to navigate the benefits and drawbacks of remote work.

item.title: Bitcoin is cratering, but a new Wall Street crypto hype is on the rise
item.description: As bitcoin dropped to its lowest price since 2024, investors flock to a new type of crypto investment linked to the hyperliquid platforms, HYPE ETFs.

item.title: South Korea nominates Han as country's first female prime minister in two decades
item.description: If approved by parliament, Han would become South Korea's first female prime minister in 20 years.

item.title: Abel goes his own way with new Berkshire investments, including billions for AI
item.description: Warren Buffett tells CNBC's Becky Quick new Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel has "launched" with his first major deal.

item.title: Bouygues-led consortium signs $23.44 billion deal to buy SFR from Altice France
item.description: If approved by regulators, the Bouygues-led acquisition would rank among the biggest European telecoms deals in recent years.

item.title: At least 12 people shot at an Ohio festival and a search for suspects is still ongoing, police say
item.description: At least 12 people shot at Ohio festival; search for suspects ongoing

item.title: How ‘Backrooms’ producer Peter Chernin thinks Hollywood needs to change
item.description: Franchise fatigue could drive an entertainment industry sea change as the appetite for fresh ideas grows and younger audiences make up more box office sales.

item.title: Russian drone hits nuclear-fuel storage facility near Chornobyl, Ukraine says
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called it an "extremely vile" attack by Russia that did not lead to a spike in radiation.

item.title: Wall Street's 'fear gauge' punches back as the 'crash up' in chip stocks finally reverses
item.description: The monster rally in semiconductor stocks hit a wall on Friday, and the VIX at last caught up with other volatility metrics.

item.title: Here are 3 forces that drove a whirlwind week for the stock market
item.description: An avalanche of selling on Friday crushed stocks. It was a dramatic reversal from record highs earlier in the week.

item.title: Best mortgage lenders for bad credit in June 2026
item.description: Having less-than-perfect credit doesn't have to be an obstacle to homeownership.

item.title: White House AI policy adviser Krishnan to leave position
item.description: Krishnan has been involved in Trump administration efforts to create a national framework for regulating AI developments.

item.title: Golden Tempo takes 158th Belmont Stakes, winning first, third legs of Triple Crown
item.description: The win made Cherie DeVaux the second woman to train a winner in the third leg of horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: Nasdaq falls 4% and suffers worst day since April 2025 as traders flee chip stocks: Live updates
item.description: All three major averages closed out a losing week.

item.title: Pope urges leaders to stop dividing as he meets migrants, youth in Spain
item.description: Speaking aboard the flight to Madrid from Rome, Leo said he was making his visit to set a good example to the world about respect for human life.

item.title: Social media bans on teens risk strengthening Big Tech's grip on the sector, Bluesky exec warns
item.description: "We're living in a world where it's almost impossible for smaller entrants to come in and build healthier spaces," Bluesky's COO, Rose Wang, told CNBC.

item.title: Google to pay SpaceX $920 million a month for compute capacity at xAI data centers
item.description: Ahead of a planned IPO, SpaceX inked a deal to rent compute capacity to Google for $920 million per month for 32 months.

item.title: Amazon unveils latest warehouse robot as tech giants continue AI layoffs
item.description: "Our experience of robots is that it's actually driven up employment rather than the reverse," Amazon executive John Boumphrey told CNBC.

item.title: AstraZeneca CEO says AI is reshaping drug development — and helping boost the odds of success
item.description: AstraZeneca CEO Pascal Soriot said AI is helping the company develop medicines faster and make smarter decisions throughout the research process.
